publishedItems.title: DOT Price Prediction: Targets $1.50-1.70 Range by April 2026 as Technical Recovery Emerges | Polkadot (DOT) shows oversold signals at $1.33 with RSI at 37.58, targeting $1.50-1.70 re... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260326-price-prediction-dot-targets-150-17...
publishedItems.description:
Original title: DOT Price Prediction: Targets $1.50-1.70 Range by April 2026 as Technical Recovery Emerges
Summary: Polkadot (DOT) shows oversold signals at $1.33 with RSI at 37.58, targeting $1.50-1.70 recovery by April 2026 as technical indicators suggest potential bounce from current support levels.
Article text: Polkadot (DOT) shows oversold signals at $1.33 with RSI at 37.58, targeting $1.50-1.70 recovery by April 2026 as technical indicators suggest potential bounce from current support levels.

• Short-term target (1 week): $1.38-1.43 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $1.50-1.70 range • Bullish breakout level: $1.63 (Upper Bollinger Band) • Critical support: $1.27-1.30

While specific analyst predictions from key opinion leaders are limited in recent trading sessions, available market analysis suggests mixed but cautiously optimistic sentiment for DOT's trajectory through 2026.

According to Coincub's January analysis, "The 2026 trajectory anticipates intense Q1 volatility peaking around the March 14 issuance cut, followed by a Q2 stabilization period ranging between $4.50 and $6.00." However, this forecast appears increasingly aggressive given current market conditions.

CoinCodex provided a more conservative outlook, projecting DOT to reach $1.52 by March 13, 2026, representing minimal upside from current levels. This aligns more closely with the technical picture we're observing today.

On-chain data from major analytics platforms suggests that DOT's current oversold conditions could present a tactical opportunity for patient investors, though broader market sentiment remains cautious.

Polkadot's technical indicators paint a picture of an oversold asset testing critical support levels. At the current price of $1.33, DOT has declined 4.19% in the past 24 hours, reflecting broader cryptocurrency market weakness.

The RSI at 37.58 indicates DOT is approaching oversold territory without quite reaching the traditional 30 threshold. This suggests potential for a technical bounce, though momentum remains bearish in the near term.

MACD analysis shows concerning signals with the MACD line at -0.0293 and histogram at 0.0000, indicating bearish momentum has stalled but hasn't yet reversed. The convergence suggests we may be approaching a potential turning point.

Bollinger Bands provide perhaps the most compelling technical signal. With DOT's %B position at 0.0243, the token is trading extremely close to the lower band at $1.32, while the upper band sits at $1.63. Historically, such extreme lower band touches often precede mean reversion moves toward the middle band ($1.47).

Key moving averages tell a mixed story. While DOT trades below most short-term averages (SMA 7: $1.41, SMA 20: $1.47), it remains above the SMA 50 at $1.43, providing some intermediate-term support. The SMA 200 at $2.41 highlights how far DOT has fallen from longer-term trends.

In a recovery scenario, DOT's initial target would be a move back toward the immediate resistance at $1.38, followed by the stronger resistance zone at $1.43. A successful reclaim of these levels could open the door to testing the Bollinger Band middle line at $1.47.

The ultimate bullish target for the next 4-6 weeks would be a challenge of the upper Bollinger Band at $1.63, representing roughly 23% upside from current levels. This Polkadot forecast assumes broader crypto market stabilization and renewed interest in layer-0 blockchain infrastructure projects.

Technical confirmation for the bullish case would require: - RSI recovery above 45 - MACD histogram turning positive - Daily volume expansion above the recent average of $11.2 million

Should current support fail, DOT faces a test of the strong support zone between $1.27-1.30. A breakdown below this level could trigger algorithmic selling and push the token toward the psychological $1.00 level.

Risk factors include: - Broader cryptocurrency market deterioration - Continued low trading volume - Failed technical bounces from oversold conditions

For traders considering DOT positions, the current technical setup offers both opportunity and risk. Conservative buyers might consider scaling into positions between $1.30-1.33, with tight stop-losses below $1.27.

More aggressive traders could wait for confirmation of a bounce, entering on a reclaim of $1.38 with targets at $1.43 and $1.47. This DOT price prediction strategy offers better risk-reward ratios but requires patience for proper setup confirmation.

This DOT price prediction suggests Polkadot is approaching an inflection point. While technical indicators show oversold conditions that historically precede bounces, the broader cryptocurrency market environment remains challenging.

The most probable scenario sees DOT consolidating between $1.30-1.50 over the next month, with potential for a push toward $1.70 if broader market conditions improve. However, a break below $1.27 could signal further weakness toward $1.00.
Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/20260326-price-prediction-dot-targets-150-170-range-by-april

publishedItems.description:
Original title: MATIC Price Prediction: Polygon Tests Critical $0.40 Resistance as Technical Indicators Signal Mixed Outlook
Summary: MATIC Price Prediction Summary • Short-term target (1 week): $0.35-$0.40 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.31-$0.45 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.43 (SMA 20) • Critical sup...
Article text: MATIC Price Prediction Summary • Short-term target (1 week): $0.35-$0.40 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.31-$0.45 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.43 (SMA 20) • Critical sup...

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.35-$0.40 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.31-$0.45 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.43 (SMA 20) • Critical support: $0.31 (Bollinger Band lower)

While specific analyst predictions are limited for the current timeframe, on-chain metrics suggest Polygon is experiencing consolidation pressure. According to available market data, MATIC showed significant momentum in early 2026 with a reported 33% surge, though current technical indicators point to a cooling-off period.

Recent analysis indicates that Polygon's ecosystem development continues to attract attention, but price action remains constrained by broader market conditions. CryptoQuant data suggests that MATIC's trading volume of $1.07 million on Binance reflects moderate investor interest, though this represents relatively low activity compared to historical peaks.

Polygon's current technical picture presents a mixed outlook with several key indicators pointing toward near-term weakness. The RSI at 38.00 sits in neutral territory but leans toward oversold conditions, suggesting potential for a bounce if buyers step in at current levels.

The MACD histogram at essentially flat (-0.0000) indicates diminishing bearish momentum, though the overall MACD remains negative at -0.0246. This suggests that while selling pressure may be easing, bullish momentum has yet to emerge.

Bollinger Bands analysis reveals MATIC trading at 0.29 of the band width, positioning it closer to the lower band ($0.31) than the upper band ($0.56). This placement typically indicates oversold conditions and potential for mean reversion toward the middle band at $0.43.

Moving averages paint a bearish picture with MATIC trading below all major timeframes. The price sits below the 7-day SMA ($0.37), 20-day SMA ($0.43), and significantly under the 200-day SMA ($0.69), confirming the longer-term downtrend remains intact.

In an optimistic scenario, MATIC price prediction suggests a recovery toward $0.43 (20-day SMA) represents the first major resistance level. A break above this level with sustained volume could target the upper Bollinger Band at $0.56, representing approximately 47% upside from current levels.

For this Polygon forecast to materialize, traders should watch for RSI breaking above 50 and MACD crossing into positive territory. Additionally, reclaiming the $0.40 psychological level would provide crucial technical confirmation.

The bearish case for MATIC centers on a breakdown below current support levels. With limited buying interest evident in the $1.07 million daily volume, a break below $0.37 could accelerate selling toward the Bollinger Band lower at $0.31.

Further downside risks include testing the psychological $0.30 level, which would represent an 18% decline from current prices. The significant gap between current price and the 200-day SMA at $0.69 suggests the broader downtrend could persist.

For risk-tolerant traders, the current MATIC price prediction suggests a potential accumulation zone between $0.31-$0.38. Conservative buyers should wait for RSI to show bullish divergence or MACD to cross positive before establishing positions.

Stop-loss placement: Position stops below the Bollinger Band lower at $0.30 to limit downside risk to approximately 20%.

This MATIC price prediction reflects a cryptocurrency at a technical crossroads. While Polygon's fundamentals remain strong with continued ecosystem development, short-term price action suggests consolidation will likely persist. The Polygon forecast indicates a trading range between $0.31-$0.45 over the next month, with the direction heavily dependent on broader cryptocurrency market sentiment.

Traders should approach MATIC with measured expectations, as the current technical setup suggests limited upside momentum until key resistance levels are convincingly broken. Risk management remains paramount given the oversold but not yet reversed technical conditions.

Disclaimer: This MATIC price prediction is for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Cryptocurrency investments carry substantial risk of loss, and past performance does not guarantee future results.

Article text: DOGE Price Prediction Summary • Short-term target (1 week): $0.10-$0.105 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.11-$0.15 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.15 • Critical support: $...

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.10-$0.105 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.11-$0.15 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.15 • Critical support: $0.09

While specific analyst predictions from major KOLs are limited in recent days, The Sports Zone published a bullish Dogecoin forecast on March 22, 2026, stating that "Dogecoin could realistically reach $0.20 to $0.50 by the end of 2026." This represents a potential 122% to 456% upside from current levels.

According to on-chain data from major analytics platforms, Dogecoin's current positioning suggests oversold conditions that historically precede recovery phases. The meme coin's technical structure indicates accumulation near key support levels.

Dogecoin's current price of $0.09 represents a -4.18% decline over the past 24 hours, with the token testing critical support levels. The RSI reading of 46.19 places DOGE in neutral territory, suggesting neither extreme oversold nor overbought conditions.

publishedItems.title: NVIDIA MIG Boosts AI Infrastructure ROI by 33% Over Time-Slicing | New NVIDIA benchmarks show Multi-Instance GPU partitioning achieves 1.00 req/s per GPU ve... https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-mig-gpu-partitioning-ai-infrastructure-benchmarks
publishedItems.description:
Original title: NVIDIA MIG Boosts AI Infrastructure ROI by 33% Over Time-Slicing
Summary: New NVIDIA benchmarks show Multi-Instance GPU partitioning achieves 1.00 req/s per GPU versus 0.76 for time-slicing in production AI workloads.
Article text: New NVIDIA benchmarks show Multi-Instance GPU partitioning achieves 1.00 req/s per GPU versus 0.76 for time-slicing in production AI workloads.

NVIDIA has released benchmark data showing its Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology delivers 33% higher throughput efficiency than software-based time-slicing for AI inference workloads—a finding that could reshape how enterprises allocate compute resources for production AI deployments.

The tests, conducted on NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPUs in a Kubernetes environment, demonstrated MIG achieving approximately 1.00 requests per second per GPU compared to 0.76 req/s for time-slicing configurations. Both approaches maintained 100% success rates with no failures during testing.

Most production AI pipelines suffer from a mismatch between model requirements and hardware allocation. Lightweight models for automatic speech recognition or text-to-speech might need only 10 GB of VRAM but occupy an entire GPU under standard Kubernetes scheduling. NVIDIA's data shows GPU compute utilization often hovers between 0-10% for these support models.

The company tested three configurations using a voice-to-voice AI pipeline: a baseline with dedicated GPUs for each model, time-slicing where ASR and TTS share a GPU through software scheduling, and MIG where hardware physically partitions the GPU into isolated instances with dedicated memory and streaming multiprocessors.

Under heavy load with 50 concurrent users over 375 seconds of sustained interaction, MIG's hardware partitioning eliminated resource contention entirely. Time-slicing showed faster individual task completion for bursty workloads—144.7ms mean TTS latency versus MIG's 168.2ms—but that 23.5ms difference becomes negligible when the LLM bottleneck accounts for roughly 9 seconds of total processing time.

The critical advantage: MIG's fault isolation prevents memory overflow in one process from crashing others sharing the card. Time-slicing's shared execution context means a fatal error propagates across all processes, potentially triggering a GPU reset.

NVIDIA recommends MIG as the default for production environments prioritizing throughput and reliability, while time-slicing suits development, CI/CD pipelines, and proof-of-concept work where minimizing hardware footprint matters more than peak performance.

For organizations running mixed AI workloads, consolidating support models onto partitioned GPUs frees entire cards for LLM instances—the actual compute bottleneck in most generative AI applications. The company has published implementation guides and YAML manifests for Kubernetes deployments through its NIM Operator framework.

publishedItems.title: NVIDIA DRIVE Radar Tech Promises 100x Data Boost for Level 4 Autonomy | NVIDIA unveils centralized radar processing on DRIVE AGX Thor, delivering 100x more senso... https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-drive-centralized-radar-level-4-autonomy
publishedItems.description:
Original title: NVIDIA DRIVE Radar Tech Promises 100x Data Boost for Level 4 Autonomy
Summary: NVIDIA unveils centralized radar processing on DRIVE AGX Thor, delivering 100x more sensor data for L4 autonomous vehicles while cutting hardware costs 30%.
Article text: NVIDIA unveils centralized radar processing on DRIVE AGX Thor, delivering 100x more sensor data for L4 autonomous vehicles while cutting hardware costs 30%.

NVIDIA just revealed a fundamental shift in how autonomous vehicles will process radar data, and the numbers are striking: 100x more information available to AI systems, 30% lower hardware costs, and 20% reduced power consumption. The company demonstrated the technology running live on its DRIVE AGX Thor platform at GTC 2026 last week.

The core problem NVIDIA is solving? Current automotive radars process data locally on each sensor, then spit out sparse point clouds to the central computer. It's like giving a photographer edge-detection outlines instead of actual photographs. Machine learning engineers have been working with the equivalent of stick figures when full portraits exist inside the sensors.

NVIDIA's approach moves all signal processing from individual radar units to the central DRIVE platform. Raw analog-to-digital converter data streams directly into system memory, where dedicated Programmable Vision Accelerator hardware handles the heavy lifting. The GPU stays free for AI workloads.

The data difference is dramatic. A single long-range radar produces 6 MB of raw ADC data per frame versus just 0.064 MB as a processed point cloud. NVIDIA's demo configuration runs five radar units—one front-facing 8T8R unit and four corner 4T4R sensors—pushing 540 MB/s aggregate versus 4.8 MB/s for traditional setups.

ChengTech, described as the first raw radar partner on the DRIVE platform, provided production-grade hardware for the GTC demonstration. The system processes all five radar feeds at 30 frames per second.

Level 4 autonomy stacks are increasingly built around large models that learn from raw sensor data. Vision-language-action architectures want dense, unprocessed signals—not pre-filtered outputs. Cameras and lidar already work this way on modern platforms. Radar has been the odd one out.

Traditional edge-processed radar also runs duty cycles below 50%, limiting frame rates to around 20 FPS. The memory constraints force sensors to discard intermediate data products like range-FFT cubes and Doppler maps. These are exactly the signal views that recent research papers have shown improve perception performance.

NVIDIA cites work from CVPR 2022 and ICCV 2023 demonstrating neural networks trained on raw ADC signals outperform those limited to point clouds. Centralized processing makes this practical at production scale.

Stripping the digital signal processors and microcontrollers from individual radar units creates simpler, cheaper sensors. NVIDIA claims over 30% unit cost reduction and roughly 20% volume decrease. The streamlined PCB design returns radar hardware to its RF fundamentals.

System-wide power consumption drops approximately 20% by leveraging the efficiency of central domain controllers rather than running multiple edge processors.

NVIDIA has been stacking autonomous driving partnerships aggressively. Hyundai and Kia expanded their strategic relationship with the company on March 16. A deal with Uber announced the same week targets robotaxi deployments across 28 cities by 2028. BYD, Geely, Isuzu, and Nissan are also integrating DRIVE platforms into upcoming vehicles.

NVDA shares traded at $175.20 as of March 24, with the company's market cap sitting at $4.44 trillion. The autonomous vehicle push represents one of several growth vectors beyond its dominant AI datacenter business.

For automakers evaluating L4 development paths, NVIDIA is positioning DRIVE Hyperion as the production-ready reference architecture. The centralized radar capability slots into an existing ecosystem that already handles cameras and lidar with the same software-defined approach. OEMs wanting to explore the technology can work with supported radar vendors through NVIDIA's partner program.

NVIDIA unveils centralized radar processing on DRIVE AGX Thor, delivering 100x more sensor data for L4 autonomous vehicles while cutting hardware costs 30%.

NVIDIA just revealed a fundamental shift in how autonomous vehicles will process radar data, and the numbers are striking: 100x more information available to AI systems, 30% lower hardware costs, and 20% reduced power consumption. The company demonstrated the technology running live on its DRIVE AGX Thor platform at GTC 2026 last week.

The core problem NVIDIA is solving? Current automotive radars process data locally on each sensor, then spit out sparse point clouds to the central computer. It's like giving a photographer edge-detection outlines instead of actual photographs. Machine learning engineers have been working with the equivalent of stick figures when full portraits exist inside the sensors.

NVIDIA's approach moves all signal processing from individual radar units to the central DRIVE platform. Raw analog-to-digital converter data streams directly into system memory, where dedicated Programmable Vision Accelerator hardware handles the heavy lifting. The GPU stays free for AI workloads.

The data difference is dramatic. A single long-range radar produces 6 MB of raw ADC data per frame versus just 0.064 MB as a processed point cloud. NVIDIA's demo configuration runs five radar units—one front-facing 8T8R unit and four corner 4T4R sensors—pushing 540 MB/s aggregate versus 4.8 MB/s for traditional setups.

ChengTech, described as the first raw radar partner on the DRIVE platform, provided production-grade hardware for the GTC demonstration. The system processes all five radar feeds at 30 frames per second.
Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-drive-centralized-radar-level-4-autonomy

publishedItems.title: LangChain Launches Shareable Skills for AI Agent Teams in LangSmith Fleet | LangSmith Fleet now lets teams create and share reusable AI agent skills, codifying compa... https://Blockchain.News/news/langchain-langsmith-fleet-shareable-skills-ai-agents
publishedItems.description:
Original title: LangChain Launches Shareable Skills for AI Agent Teams in LangSmith Fleet
Summary: LangSmith Fleet now lets teams create and share reusable AI agent skills, codifying company knowledge for specialized tasks across workspaces.
Article text: LangSmith Fleet now lets teams create and share reusable AI agent skills, codifying company knowledge for specialized tasks across workspaces.

LangChain has rolled out shareable skills for its LangSmith Fleet platform, giving teams a way to codify institutional knowledge and distribute it across AI agents without manual coordination.

The feature addresses a persistent problem in enterprise AI deployment: agents can reason well but lack the domain-specific knowledge that makes them actually useful. A customer support bot that doesn't understand your SLA tiers treats every ticket identically. The expertise that differentiates good from mediocre agent performance typically lives in employees' heads or scattered across Notion pages and Slack threads.

A skill functions as a persistent briefing document attached to an agent—instructions and domain knowledge that shape behavior for specific tasks. Examples include ticket triage protocols based on SLA tiers, brand voice guidelines for writing agents, or step-by-step refund workflows.

Agents load skills only when relevant, keeping responses focused. Write the knowledge once, share it to your workspace, and every agent can access it. When employees leave, their documented expertise stays.

Fleet offers multiple approaches to building skills:

AI-assisted creation lets users describe what they want in chat. Fleet asks clarifying questions and generates the skill. Previous conversations can be converted to reusable skills at any time.

Auto-generation during agent setup creates relevant skills automatically when you build a new agent. These start private but can be shared to the workspace.

Templates cover common use cases—account briefing, SEO audits, deep research—out of the box.

Manual authoring gives teams full control when they want it.

Skills aren't locked into Fleet. The LangSmith CLI pulls any workspace skill into local development environments with a single command. That same knowledge becomes available to Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or custom agents without copy-pasting.

LangChain flagged two near-term additions: version pinning with rollback capability (so a bad skill update doesn't break production agents) and multi-owner permissions for collaborative maintenance.

For teams building production AI systems, the update signals LangChain's push toward enterprise collaboration features. The quality gap between generally capable agents and reliably good ones increasingly comes down to instruction quality—and that's exactly what skills are designed to address.

publishedItems.title: Tether's QVAC Brings Billion-Parameter AI Training to Smartphones | Tether launches BitNet LoRA framework enabling AI model fine-tuning on consumer devices,... https://Blockchain.News/news/tether-qvac-bitnet-lora-smartphone-ai-training
publishedItems.description:
Original title: Tether's QVAC Brings Billion-Parameter AI Training to Smartphones
Summary: Tether launches BitNet LoRA framework enabling AI model fine-tuning on consumer devices, slashing VRAM usage by up to 78% versus traditional models.
Article text: Tether launches BitNet LoRA framework enabling AI model fine-tuning on consumer devices, slashing VRAM usage by up to 78% versus traditional models.

Tether just made billion-parameter AI models trainable on your phone. The stablecoin giant's QVAC division launched what it claims is the first cross-platform LoRA fine-tuning framework for Microsoft's BitNet architecture, enabling AI development on consumer hardware that previously required enterprise-grade NVIDIA systems.

The numbers are striking. A 125-million-parameter BitNet model can be fine-tuned in roughly 10 minutes on a Samsung S25 using a biomedical dataset of about 300 documents. Scale that up to a 1-billion-parameter model, and you're looking at just over an hour on the same device. Tether's team pushed an iPhone 16 to fine-tune models up to 13 billion parameters.

The real story here is efficiency. Benchmarks show BitNet-1B uses up to 77.8% less VRAM than Gemma-3-1B and 65.6% less than Qwen3-0.6B during both inference and fine-tuning. That memory headroom means larger models can run on hardware that would've been dismissed as inadequate six months ago.

GPU inference on mobile devices clocked between two and eleven times faster than CPU performance, according to Tether's testing on Adreno, Mali, and Apple Bionic GPUs. The framework also extends LoRA fine-tuning to non-NVIDIA hardware for the first time—AMD, Intel, and Apple Silicon are all supported.

This release follows Tether's broader push beyond stablecoins. QVAC Fabric, launched as open-source software, represents the company's bet that decentralized AI infrastructure can compete with cloud giants. The timing is notable: Tether announced on March 25 that it hired a Big Four accounting firm for its first USDT audit, suggesting the $184 billion stablecoin issuer is working to shore up credibility across all business lines.

"When training large language models depends on centralized infrastructure, innovation becomes stagnant," CEO Paolo Ardoino said. "The era of Stable Intelligence has just begun."

The framework opens doors for federated learning—training AI across distributed devices while keeping sensitive data local. For developers priced out of cloud AI, that's a meaningful shift. Whether Tether can build a viable AI ecosystem alongside its stablecoin dominance remains the open question.

Full technical documentation, including adapters and cross-platform binaries, is available on Hugging Face.

The stablecoin giant's QVAC division launched what it claims is the first cross-platform LoRA fine-tuning framework for Microsoft's BitNet architecture, enabling AI development on consumer hardware that previously required enterprise-grade NVIDIA systems.

The numbers are striking. A 125-million-parameter BitNet model can be fine-tuned in roughly 10 minutes on a Samsung S25 using a biomedical dataset of about 300 documents. Scale that up to a 1-billion-parameter model, and you're looking at just over an hour on the same device. Tether's team pushed an iPhone 16 to fine-tune models up to 13 billion parameters.

The real story here is efficiency. Benchmarks show BitNet-1B uses up to 77.8% less VRAM than Gemma-3-1B and 65.6% less than Qwen3-0.6B during both inference and fine-tuning. That memory headroom means larger models can run on hardware that would've been dismissed as inadequate six months ago.

GPU inference on mobile devices clocked between two and eleven times faster than CPU performance, according to Tether's testing on Adreno, Mali, and Apple Bionic GPUs. The framework also extends LoRA fine-tuning to non-NVIDIA hardware for the first time—AMD, Intel, and Apple Silicon are all supported.

This release follows Tether's broader push beyond stablecoins. QVAC Fabric, launched as open-source software, represents the company's bet that decentralized AI infrastructure can compete with cloud giants. The timing is notable: Tether announced on March 25 that it hired a Big Four accounting firm for its first USDT audit, suggesting the $184 billion stablecoin issuer is working to shore up credibility across all business lines.

"When training large language models depends on centralized infrastructure, innovation becomes stagnant," CEO Paolo Ardoino said. "The era of Stable Intelligence has just begun."

The framework opens doors for federated learning—training AI across distributed devices while keeping sensitive data local. For developers priced out of cloud AI, that's a meaningful shift. Whether Tether can build a viable AI ecosystem alongside its stablecoin dominance remains the open question.

Full technical documentation, including adapters and cross-platform binaries, is available on Hugging Face.

publishedItems.title: Circle Brings USDCx Stablecoin to Movement L1 Blockchain | Movement becomes the fifth blockchain to integrate Circle's xReserve infrastructure, enab... https://Blockchain.News/news/circle-usdcx-movement-l1-blockchain-xreserve
publishedItems.description:
Original title: Circle Brings USDCx Stablecoin to Movement L1 Blockchain
Summary: Movement becomes the fifth blockchain to integrate Circle's xReserve infrastructure, enabling USDC-backed stablecoin access across 30+ chains without third-party bridges.
Article text: Movement becomes the fifth blockchain to integrate Circle's xReserve infrastructure, enabling USDC-backed stablecoin access across 30+ chains without third-party bridges.

Circle has expanded its xReserve infrastructure to Movement, a layer-1 blockchain built on the Move Virtual Machine, marking the fifth network integration for the USDC-backed stablecoin system. The deployment gives Movement users access to USDCx—a dollar stablecoin backed 1:1 by USDC reserves—with native cross-chain interoperability spanning more than 30 supported blockchains.

The integration matters because it bypasses third-party bridge protocols entirely. Through Circle's CCTP (Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol) and Gateway products, USDCx on Movement can move to USDC on other chains without the security risks that have plagued bridge exploits over the past two years.

Unlike wrapped tokens that rely on external custodians, xReserve functions as non-custodial smart contract infrastructure. It handles minting and provides deposit attestations for USDCx, meaning the backing verification happens on-chain rather than through trust assumptions about a bridge operator's reserves.

Movement joins Aleo, Canton, Cardano, and Stacks as xReserve-connected networks. Each deployment allows USDCx to flow between compatible chains while maintaining its 1:1 USDC backing—currently supported by Circle's $78.7 billion stablecoin market cap.

Movement differentiates itself through the Move programming language, originally developed at Meta for the abandoned Diem project. The language was designed specifically to prevent common smart contract vulnerabilities around asset handling. Combined with EVM compatibility and what Movement describes as "near-instant finality," the chain is positioning itself for DeFi applications where security and speed both matter.

The mainnet USDCx contract sits at address 0xba11833544a2f99eec743f41a228ca6ffa7f13c3b6b04681d5a79a8b75ff225e for anyone looking to verify or integrate.

Circle and Movement are pitching several applications: DeFi liquidity provision without bridge dependencies, cross-border remittances with lower fees than traditional rails, and access to tokenized real-world assets like Treasury bills. The neobank angle—connecting USDCx balances to debit card spending—represents the kind of fiat on/off-ramp integration that could drive actual retail usage.

Whether Movement captures meaningful DeFi activity remains to be seen. The chain launches with several apps claiming Day 1 USDCx integration, though specific names weren't disclosed in the announcement.

For traders and developers, the practical takeaway is straightforward: Movement now has a stablecoin with legitimate backing and cross-chain liquidity access. That's table stakes for any L1 hoping to attract serious DeFi capital, and Circle continues expanding xReserve's footprint across the multi-chain landscape.

publishedItems.title: NVIDIA Releases Proteina-Complexa AI Model for Drug Discovery | NVIDIA's new generative AI model designs protein binders with nano- and picomolar binding... https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-proteina-complexa-ai-protein-binder-drug-discovery
publishedItems.description:
Original title: NVIDIA Releases Proteina-Complexa AI Model for Drug Discovery
Summary: NVIDIA's new generative AI model designs protein binders with nano- and picomolar binding affinities, validated across 133 targets with pharma partners.
Article text: NVIDIA's new generative AI model designs protein binders with nano- and picomolar binding affinities, validated across 133 targets with pharma partners.

NVIDIA has released Proteina-Complexa, a generative AI model capable of designing de novo protein binders and enzymes—a development that could accelerate drug discovery timelines for pharmaceutical companies and biotech startups alike.

The model represents NVIDIA's latest push into computational biology, an area where the company has been aggressively expanding beyond its core GPU business. For investors tracking NVIDIA's diversification strategy, this release signals continued commitment to healthcare AI applications.

Protein binders are molecules designed to attach to specific targets—think of them as custom-built keys for biological locks. Creating them traditionally requires extensive trial and error in wet labs, costing pharmaceutical companies millions and taking years.

Proteina-Complexa generates both the 3D structure and amino acid sequence simultaneously, a co-design approach that eliminates the fragmented workflow most computational tools require. The model was trained on over 1 million curated structures from major protein databases including the Protein Data Bank and AlphaFold Protein Structure Database.

NVIDIA didn't just release this into the wild untested. Working with Manifold Bio, Novo Nordisk, Viva Biotech, Duke University, and the University of Cambridge, the team put roughly 1 million binder candidates through experimental testing against 133 distinct protein targets.

The results: generated proteins expressed well with high folding stability, and the model produced binders with nano- and picomolar affinities for most targets. That's the binding strength range where therapeutics become viable.

One standout result involved designing binders for sugar molecules on red blood cell surfaces—historically considered nearly impossible due to carbohydrates' highly polar nature. Four of 24 candidates showed stronger agglutination signals than natural proteins currently used in laboratories.

The use cases span oncology, immunology, and neurology for protein targets, plus targeted drug delivery and biosensor development for small molecules. Enzyme design capabilities open doors for industrial biocatalysis and environmental remediation.

NVIDIA has open-sourced the code, model checkpoints, and datasets—a strategic move that could establish Proteina-Complexa as an industry standard while driving demand for NVIDIA hardware. The model requires at least one A100, H100, or newer GPU to run.

For biotech investors, the collaboration roster matters. Novo Nordisk's involvement suggests big pharma sees genuine potential here, not just academic curiosity. The technology targets a real bottleneck in drug development where computational efficiency could translate directly to faster time-to-market for new therapeutics.

Proteina-Complexa generates both the 3D structure and amino acid sequence simultaneously, a co-design approach that eliminates the fragmented workflow most computational tools require. The model was trained on over 1 million curated structures from major protein databases including the Protein Data Bank and AlphaFold Protein Structure Database.

NVIDIA didn't just release this into the wild untested. Working with Manifold Bio, Novo Nordisk, Viva Biotech, Duke University, and the University of Cambridge, the team put roughly 1 million binder candidates through experimental testing against 133 distinct protein targets.

The results: generated proteins expressed well with high folding stability, and the model produced binders with nano- and picomolar affinities for most targets. That's the binding strength range where therapeutics become viable.

One standout result involved designing binders for sugar molecules on red blood cell surfaces—historically considered nearly impossible due to carbohydrates' highly polar nature. Four of 24 candidates showed stronger agglutination signals than natural proteins currently used in laboratories.

The use cases span oncology, immunology, and neurology for protein targets, plus targeted drug delivery and biosensor development for small molecules. Enzyme design capabilities open doors for industrial biocatalysis and environmental remediation.

publishedItems.articleText:
NVIDIA's new generative AI model designs protein binders with nano- and picomolar binding affinities, validated across 133 targets with pharma partners.

NVIDIA has released Proteina-Complexa, a generative AI model capable of designing de novo protein binders and enzymes—a development that could accelerate drug discovery timelines for pharmaceutical companies and biotech startups alike.

The model represents NVIDIA's latest push into computational biology, an area where the company has been aggressively expanding beyond its core GPU business. For investors tracking NVIDIA's diversification strategy, this release signals continued commitment to healthcare AI applications.

Protein binders are molecules designed to attach to specific targets—think of them as custom-built keys for biological locks. Creating them traditionally requires extensive trial and error in wet labs, costing pharmaceutical companies millions and taking years.

Proteina-Complexa generates both the 3D structure and amino acid sequence simultaneously, a co-design approach that eliminates the fragmented workflow most computational tools require. The model was trained on over 1 million curated structures from major protein databases including the Protein Data Bank and AlphaFold Protein Structure Database.

NVIDIA didn't just release this into the wild untested. Working with Manifold Bio, Novo Nordisk, Viva Biotech, Duke University, and the University of Cambridge, the team put roughly 1 million binder candidates through experimental testing against 133 distinct protein targets.

The results: generated proteins expressed well with high folding stability, and the model produced binders with nano- and picomolar affinities for most targets. That's the binding strength range where therapeutics become viable.

One standout result involved designing binders for sugar molecules on red blood cell surfaces—historically considered nearly impossible due to carbohydrates' highly polar nature. Four of 24 candidates showed stronger agglutination signals than natural proteins currently used in laboratories.

The use cases span oncology, immunology, and neurology for protein targets, plus targeted drug delivery and biosensor development for small molecules. Enzyme design capabilities open doors for industrial biocatalysis and environmental remediation.

NVIDIA has open-sourced the code, model checkpoints, and datasets—a strategic move that could establish Proteina-Complexa as an industry standard while driving demand for NVIDIA hardware. The model requires at least one A100, H100, or newer GPU to run.

For biotech investors, the collaboration roster matters. Novo Nordisk's involvement suggests big pharma sees genuine potential here, not just academic curiosity. The technology targets a real bottleneck in drug development where computational efficiency could translate directly to faster time-to-market for new therapeutics.

publishedItems.description:
Original title: SHIB Price Prediction: Technical Consolidation Suggests Limited Movement Through April 2026
Summary: Shiba Inu shows neutral RSI at 56.81 with bearish MACD momentum. Technical analysis suggests SHIB may remain range-bound near current $0.0000062 levels through April.
Article text: Shiba Inu shows neutral RSI at 56.81 with bearish MACD momentum. Technical analysis suggests SHIB may remain range-bound near current $0.0000062 levels through April.

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.0000060-$0.0000065 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.0000058-$0.0000070 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.0000070 • Critical support: $0.0000058

While specific analyst predictions are limited in the current market environment, on-chain metrics suggest SHIB is experiencing a period of technical consolidation. According to recent trading data from Binance, SHIB has maintained relatively stable price action with modest daily volatility. The lack of significant institutional commentary reflects the broader meme coin market's current state, where traders are focusing more on fundamental blockchain developments rather than speculative price movements.

Data platforms show that SHIB's trading volume of $7.7 million over the past 24 hours indicates moderate retail interest, though this remains below the elevated volumes seen during previous rally periods.

The current technical landscape for Shiba Inu presents a mixed but primarily neutral outlook. The RSI indicator sits at 56.81, placing SHIB firmly in neutral territory without overbought or oversold conditions. This suggests balanced buying and selling pressure in the near term.

However, the MACD histogram reading of 0.0000 with bearish momentum signals potential downside pressure. This bearish momentum indicator suggests that while SHIB isn't experiencing dramatic selling, the underlying trend momentum favors sellers over buyers currently.

The Bollinger Band position at 0.8492 indicates SHIB is trading near the upper band resistance, which historically suggests limited upside potential in the immediate term. The stochastic indicators show %K at 71.95 and %D at 57.56, reflecting momentum that's approaching overbought levels but hasn't reached extreme readings.

In a bullish scenario, SHIB could target the $0.0000070 level, representing approximately 13% upside from current levels. This Shiba Inu forecast would require breaking above current Bollinger Band resistance and seeing RSI move into the 65-70 range with confirmed buying volume. The bullish case strengthens if SHIB can maintain above $0.0000065 for consecutive trading sessions, potentially attracting momentum traders back into the meme coin sector.

Technical confirmation would need to include MACD histogram turning positive and sustained trading volume above the current $7.7 million daily average.

The bearish case for this SHIB price prediction targets support around $0.0000058, representing potential downside of approximately 6-8% from current levels. Given the bearish MACD momentum, a break below immediate support could accelerate selling toward stronger support levels. Risk factors include broader cryptocurrency market weakness, reduced meme coin trading interest, or technical breakdown below key support levels.

A decisive break below $0.0000058 could open the door to further downside toward $0.0000055 levels.

Based on current technical conditions, potential SHIB buyers should consider a scaled entry approach. Initial positions could be considered near $0.0000060-$0.0000062 levels, with additional accumulation opportunities on any dips toward $0.0000058 support.

Stop-loss levels should be set below $0.0000055 to manage downside risk effectively. For swing traders, the current consolidation pattern suggests waiting for clearer directional signals before committing significant capital.

Risk management remains crucial given SHIB's inherent volatility and the current bearish MACD momentum. Position sizing should reflect the speculative nature of meme coin investments.

This SHIB price prediction suggests Shiba Inu is likely to remain range-bound between $0.0000058-$0.0000070 through April 2026, with a slight bias toward the lower end of this range given current bearish momentum indicators. The neutral RSI provides flexibility for movement in either direction, but the technical setup favors patience over aggressive positioning.

Confidence level for this Shiba Inu forecast is moderate, as meme coin price movements can be influenced by social media sentiment and broader market narratives that technical analysis cannot fully capture. Traders should monitor volume patterns and broader cryptocurrency market trends for confirmation of directional moves.

Disclaimer: Cryptocurrency price predictions are speculative and should not constitute financial advice. SHIB and other digital assets carry significant risk of loss. Always conduct your own research and consider your risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

publishedItems.description:
Original title: WLD Price Prediction: Worldcoin Eyes $0.35 Recovery by April 2026
Summary: Worldcoin (WLD) trades at $0.32 with neutral RSI at 39.27. Technical analysis suggests potential recovery to $0.35 resistance level within 4-6 weeks if current support holds.
Article text: Worldcoin (WLD) trades at $0.32 with neutral RSI at 39.27. Technical analysis suggests potential recovery to $0.35 resistance level within 4-6 weeks if current support holds.

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.33 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.30-$0.35 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.41 (Upper Bollinger Band) • Critical support: $0.31

While specific analyst predictions for Worldcoin are limited in recent market commentary, on-chain metrics suggest a consolidation phase for WLD. The broader cryptocurrency market sentiment remains influenced by institutional forecasts, with Standard Chartered maintaining their $200,000 Bitcoin target for end of 2025, which could provide tailwinds for alternative cryptocurrencies like Worldcoin.

According to on-chain data from major exchanges, Worldcoin's trading volume of $10.1 million on Binance indicates moderate institutional interest, though this represents a relatively quiet period for the token compared to its historical peaks.

The current WLD price prediction is heavily influenced by several key technical indicators painting a mixed but cautiously optimistic picture. Worldcoin's RSI at 39.27 sits in neutral territory, avoiding oversold conditions while suggesting room for upward movement without immediate overbought concerns.

The MACD analysis reveals bearish momentum with a histogram reading of 0.0000, indicating that selling pressure has stabilized but hasn't yet reversed to bullish territory. This suggests Worldcoin may continue sideways consolidation before establishing a clear directional trend.

Worldcoin's position within the Bollinger Bands shows the token trading at 0.2277 of the band width, closer to the lower band at $0.30 than the upper resistance at $0.41. This positioning typically suggests potential for mean reversion toward the middle band at $0.35.

The moving average structure presents a bearish longer-term outlook, with WLD trading below all major moving averages. The SMA 200 at $0.71 represents a significant overhead resistance level that would need substantial bullish catalyst to overcome.

In an optimistic Worldcoin forecast, WLD could target the immediate resistance at $0.33, followed by a move toward the SMA 20 level at $0.35. A breakout above this level would open the path to the upper Bollinger Band at $0.41, representing a 28% upside potential from current levels.

Technical confirmation for this bullish scenario would require RSI moving above 50, MACD histogram turning positive, and sustained trading volume above the recent average. The Stochastic indicators at %K 21.11 and %D 16.89 suggest oversold conditions that could support a bounce.

The downside WLD price prediction focuses on the strong support level at $0.31. A break below this level could trigger further selling toward the lower Bollinger Band at $0.30, representing a 6% decline from current prices.

Risk factors include the bearish moving average alignment, with all short and medium-term averages trading below longer-term trends. Additionally, the MACD bearish momentum could intensify if broader cryptocurrency market sentiment deteriorates.

Current technical levels suggest a strategic entry approach for Worldcoin. Conservative investors might consider accumulating near the $0.31 support level with a stop-loss below $0.30 to limit downside risk.

More aggressive traders could enter at current levels around $0.32, targeting the $0.33-$0.35 resistance zone for a 3-9% potential gain. The risk-reward ratio appears favorable given the proximity to established support levels.

Position sizing should account for Worldcoin's daily ATR of $0.02, indicating moderate volatility that requires appropriate risk management. A 2-3% portfolio allocation would be prudent given the current technical uncertainty.

The WLD price prediction for the coming month suggests a cautiously optimistic outlook with a target range of $0.30-$0.35. Technical indicators support a potential recovery to the $0.35 level, representing the SMA 20 resistance, within the next 4-6 weeks.

However, traders should remain vigilant of the bearish longer-term moving average structure and monitor broader cryptocurrency market conditions. The Worldcoin forecast carries moderate confidence given the neutral RSI and stabilizing MACD conditions.

Disclaimer: Cryptocurrency price predictions are highly speculative and subject to extreme volatility. This analysis is for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Always conduct your own research and never invest more than you can afford to lose.

Original title: SUI Price Prediction: Breaking Above $1.00 Could Target $1.15 by April 2026
Summary: Sui (SUI) trades at $0.97 with neutral RSI at 49.43. Technical analysis suggests potential breakout above $0.99 resistance could drive SUI toward $1.15, but critical support at $0.91 remains key.
Article text: Sui (SUI) trades at $0.97 with neutral RSI at 49.43. Technical analysis suggests potential breakout above $0.99 resistance could drive SUI toward $1.15, but critical support at $0.91 remains key.

Sui (SUI) has been consolidating near the $0.97 level as March 2026 draws to a close, presenting an intriguing technical setup for traders and investors. With the token showing modest gains of 1.60% in the last 24 hours, our comprehensive SUI price prediction analyzes key support and resistance levels that could determine the next major move.

• Short-term target (1 week): $1.02-$1.05 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.91-$1.15 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.99 • Critical support: $0.91

While specific analyst predictions are limited in recent days, Gordon Frayne provided an updated Sui forecast in his January 2026 analysis, discussing both bullish and bearish scenarios along with critical support and resistance zones. According to on-chain data platforms, SUI's technical structure suggests the token is at a pivotal juncture.

The lack of overwhelming bearish or bullish sentiment from key opinion leaders indicates that SUI price prediction remains data-driven rather than sentiment-led, which could work in favor of technical analysis accuracy.

SUI's current technical picture presents a mixed but cautiously optimistic outlook. Trading at $0.97, the token sits just above its 20-day simple moving average of $0.96, indicating short-term stability.

The RSI reading of 49.43 places SUI in neutral territory, suggesting neither overbought nor oversold conditions. This balanced momentum indicator provides room for movement in either direction, making the upcoming price action critical for determining trend direction.

The MACD histogram at 0.0000 with both MACD and signal lines at -0.0077 indicates bearish momentum has stalled, potentially setting up for a reversal if buying pressure emerges.

SUI's position within the Bollinger Bands shows the token trading at 0.51 relative position, essentially at the middle band ($0.96). The upper band at $1.06 represents the immediate upside target, while the lower band at $0.87 provides downside reference.

Key resistance levels emerge at $0.98 (immediate) and $0.99 (strong), while support zones are identified at $0.94 (immediate) and $0.91 (strong).

A decisive break above $0.99 resistance could trigger a measured move toward $1.06, representing the upper Bollinger Band. If momentum sustains beyond this level, the next logical target sits around $1.15, derived from extending the recent trading range.

For this bullish Sui forecast to materialize, SUI would need to see: - Volume surge above the 24-hour average of $24.6 million - RSI climbing above 60 to confirm momentum - MACD histogram turning positive

Should SUI fail to hold current levels, a break below $0.94 support could accelerate selling toward the strong support zone at $0.91. A breach of this level might target the lower Bollinger Band near $0.87.

Risk factors include: - Broader crypto market weakness - Failure to reclaim $0.99 resistance on multiple attempts - RSI dropping below 40

Based on current technical analysis, potential entry strategies include:

Conservative Approach: Wait for a pullback to $0.94-$0.95 support zone with a stop-loss at $0.90. This provides a favorable risk-to-reward ratio targeting $1.02-$1.05.

Aggressive Approach: Enter on a breakout above $0.99 with confirmation volume, targeting $1.06 initially and $1.15 on extension. Place stop-loss at $0.96.

The daily ATR of $0.05 suggests moderate volatility, making SUI suitable for both swing trading and longer-term positioning with appropriate risk management.

Our SUI price prediction suggests the token is poised for a potential breakout, with the $0.99 resistance level serving as the key catalyst. While technical indicators remain neutral, the setup favors upside movement toward $1.06-$1.15 if buying momentum emerges.

However, failure to break resistance could lead to consolidation or decline toward $0.91 support. Traders should monitor volume and RSI confirmation for optimal entry timing.
Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/20260325-price-prediction-target-sui-breaking-above-100-could-115-by

publishedItems.description:
Original title: AVAX Price Prediction: Neutral Consolidation Targets $10.35 Resistance by April 2026
Summary: Avalanche trades at $9.70 with neutral RSI at 52.68. Technical analysis suggests AVAX could test $10.35 Bollinger Band resistance within 2-4 weeks if momentum builds.
Article text: Avalanche trades at $9.70 with neutral RSI at 52.68. Technical analysis suggests AVAX could test $10.35 Bollinger Band resistance within 2-4 weeks if momentum builds.

Avalanche (AVAX) is trading at $9.70 as of March 25, 2026, showing modest gains of 1.25% in the past 24 hours. With technical indicators painting a mixed picture, this AVAX price prediction examines the cryptocurrency's potential trajectory over the coming weeks.

• Short-term target (1 week): $9.84 - $10.35 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $8.72 - $10.35 range • Bullish breakout level: $10.35 (Upper Bollinger Band) • Critical support: $9.21 (Strong Support Level)

While specific analyst predictions are limited in recent days, available on-chain metrics suggest Avalanche is experiencing a period of consolidation. According to recent analysis from Cilinix Crypto (@CilinixCrypto) published in February 2026, there are "potential factors that could lead to an increase in AVAX's price," though specific price targets from this analysis were not detailed in the available data.

The lack of fresh analyst commentary suggests the market is waiting for clearer directional signals before major institutions and key opinion leaders commit to specific Avalanche forecasts.

The current technical picture for AVAX presents a neutral stance with slight bullish undertones. The RSI reading of 52.68 places Avalanche in neutral territory, neither overbought nor oversold, suggesting balanced buying and selling pressure.

Moving Average Analysis: AVAX is trading above most short-term moving averages, with the current price of $9.70 sitting above the 7-day SMA ($9.45), 20-day SMA ($9.53), and 50-day SMA ($9.26). However, the token remains significantly below the 200-day SMA at $16.04, indicating the longer-term trend remains bearish.

MACD Momentum: The MACD histogram at 0.0000 suggests bearish momentum, with the MACD line ($0.0292) converging with its signal line ($0.0292). This convergence often precedes a directional move, though the current reading doesn't clearly favor either bulls or bears.

Bollinger Bands Position: With AVAX positioned at 0.60 within the Bollinger Bands (where 0 represents the lower band and 1 the upper band), the token has room to move higher toward the upper band at $10.35 before encountering significant resistance.

In the optimistic scenario for this AVAX price prediction, Avalanche could target the immediate resistance at $9.84, followed by the stronger resistance level at $9.99. The ultimate bullish target sits at the upper Bollinger Band of $10.35, representing a potential 6.7% upside from current levels.

Technical confirmation for bullish momentum would require the RSI to break above 60 and the MACD histogram to turn positive. A daily close above $9.84 would likely trigger algorithmic buying and could accelerate the move toward $10.35.

The bearish case for Avalanche sees initial support at $9.45 (7-day SMA), followed by stronger support at $9.21. A break below these levels could trigger a deeper correction toward the lower Bollinger Band at $8.72, representing a potential 10% decline.

Risk factors include the significant gap between current prices and the 200-day moving average, suggesting the longer-term downtrend remains intact. Additionally, the MACD's current bearish momentum could intensify if selling pressure increases.

Based on current technical levels, conservative entry points for AVAX include the $9.45-$9.53 range, which aligns with key moving average support levels. More aggressive traders might consider entries on any pullback to the $9.35 daily low.

Stop-loss suggestions: Place stops below $9.21 to limit downside risk to approximately 5% from current levels. For shorter-term trades targeting the $10.35 resistance, a tighter stop at $9.35 could be appropriate.

Risk management: Given the neutral technical picture, position sizing should be conservative. The 14-day ATR of $0.46 suggests daily volatility of approximately 4.7%, which should inform position sizing decisions.

This AVAX price prediction suggests Avalanche is likely to trade within the $8.72-$10.35 range over the next month, with a slight bias toward testing the upper resistance. The neutral RSI and converging MACD indicate a potential breakout in either direction, making this a critical juncture for AVAX holders.

The most probable scenario sees Avalanche testing the $10.35 resistance within 2-4 weeks, with success depending on broader cryptocurrency market conditions and any fundamental developments within the Avalanche ecosystem. However, failure to hold above $9.21 support could trigger a deeper correction.

Disclaimer: Cryptocurrency price predictions are inherently speculative and should not constitute financial advice. Always conduct your own research and consider your risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

publishedItems.description:
Original title: DOT Price Prediction: Polkadot Eyes $1.44 Recovery Amid Technical Consolidation
Summary: DOT price prediction shows potential recovery to $1.44 resistance as Polkadot trades near Bollinger Band support at $1.39, with key levels defining short-term direction.
Article text: DOT price prediction shows potential recovery to $1.44 resistance as Polkadot trades near Bollinger Band support at $1.39, with key levels defining short-term direction.

Polkadot (DOT) is currently trading at $1.39, down 2.05% in the past 24 hours, as the cryptocurrency navigates a critical technical juncture. With DOT positioned near its lower Bollinger Band and showing mixed momentum signals, traders are watching key levels that could determine the next directional move for this major blockchain platform.

• Short-term target (1 week): $1.44 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $1.33-$1.48 range • Bullish breakout level: $1.48 • Critical support: $1.35

While specific analyst predictions for DOT are limited in recent trading sessions, on-chain data suggests mixed sentiment around Polkadot's current price action. The lack of prominent KOL commentary may indicate market participants are waiting for clearer technical signals before making bold predictions.

According to technical analysis platforms, DOT's current positioning near key support levels warrants close monitoring, particularly given the broader cryptocurrency market's influence on individual altcoin performance.

The current DOT price prediction is heavily influenced by several key technical indicators painting a nuanced picture. Polkadot's RSI sits at 41.52, placing it firmly in neutral territory and suggesting neither oversold nor overbought conditions. This neutral RSI reading indicates potential for movement in either direction based on market catalysts.

The MACD histogram shows a bearish momentum reading at 0.0000, with both the MACD line and signal line converging at -0.0187. This convergence suggests weakening bearish momentum and potential for a momentum shift.

Polkadot's Bollinger Band positioning is particularly noteworthy, with DOT trading at just 0.1413 of the band width, placing it very close to the lower band at $1.35. The middle band (20-day SMA) sits at $1.48, while the upper band extends to $1.62.

Moving averages present a mixed outlook for this Polkadot forecast. The short-term EMAs (12-period at $1.46 and 26-period at $1.48) remain above the current price, indicating potential resistance. However, the 50-day SMA at $1.43 sits relatively close to current levels, suggesting possible support convergence.

In the optimistic case for DOT price prediction, Polkadot could target the immediate resistance at $1.42, followed by the stronger resistance zone around $1.44. A sustained break above $1.44 could open the path toward the 20-day SMA at $1.48, which represents the Bollinger Band middle line.

For this bullish scenario to materialize, DOT would need to see increased trading volume beyond the current $11.97 million 24-hour volume and RSI movement above 50. The stochastic indicators (%K at 8.94 and %D at 7.15) are currently oversold, which could support a bounce from these levels.

The bearish case for this Polkadot forecast centers around a break below the critical support at $1.36, which represents the 24-hour low. A decisive move below this level could target the lower Bollinger Band at $1.35, with further downside potential toward $1.33.

Risk factors include the significant gap between current price and the 200-day SMA at $2.42, indicating a longer-term downtrend that could continue to pressure prices. The negative MACD readings also support potential continued weakness.

Based on current technical levels, potential entry points for DOT include the $1.36-$1.37 range, which offers proximity to key support with defined risk parameters. A more aggressive entry could target current levels around $1.39, with the expectation of a bounce toward $1.44 resistance.

Stop-loss placement should consider the lower Bollinger Band at $1.35, with a break below this level invalidating the near-term bullish thesis. For risk management, position sizing should account for the Daily ATR of $0.08, indicating moderate volatility expectations.

Conservative traders might wait for a confirmed break above $1.42 before entering, as this would signal the beginning of a potential recovery toward higher resistance levels.

This DOT price prediction suggests Polkadot is at a critical juncture, with technical indicators showing mixed signals that could resolve in either direction. The proximity to Bollinger Band support and oversold stochastic readings provide some bullish potential, while negative MACD momentum and distance from key moving averages present headwinds.

The most probable scenario sees DOT trading within the $1.33-$1.48 range over the coming weeks, with breaks outside this range likely to determine the next significant directional move. Traders should monitor volume confirmation and broader market sentiment as key factors in this Polkadot forecast.

Disclaimer: Cryptocurrency price predictions are highly speculative and subject to extreme volatility. This analysis is for educational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Always conduct your own research and consider your risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

publishedItems.description:
Original title: MATIC Price Prediction: Polygon Eyes $0.45 Recovery by April 2026 Amid Technical Consolidation
Summary: Polygon (MATIC) trades at $0.38 with neutral RSI at 38. Technical analysis suggests potential recovery to $0.45 resistance level within 4-6 weeks if key support holds.
Article text: Polygon (MATIC) trades at $0.38 with neutral RSI at 38. Technical analysis suggests potential recovery to $0.45 resistance level within 4-6 weeks if key support holds.

Polygon (MATIC) continues to navigate choppy waters as it trades at $0.38, down 0.29% in the last 24 hours. With the token sitting well below its key moving averages and showing mixed technical signals, this comprehensive MATIC price prediction examines the critical levels that could determine Polygon's trajectory in the coming weeks.

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.39-$0.42 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.35-$0.45 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.45 (SMA 50 resistance) • Critical support: $0.31 (Bollinger Band lower boundary)

While specific analyst predictions are limited in recent days, on-chain metrics suggest Polygon remains in a consolidation phase. The lack of fresh institutional commentary reflects the broader market uncertainty surrounding Layer 2 scaling solutions as Ethereum's ecosystem continues to evolve.

According to technical data from major exchanges, MATIC's current positioning below all major moving averages indicates the token needs significant momentum to break out of its current range. The 24-hour trading volume of $1,074,371 on Binance suggests moderate interest but lacks the conviction needed for a decisive move in either direction.

The current technical picture for Polygon presents a mixed but slightly bearish outlook. With MATIC trading at $0.38, the token sits significantly below its 20-day SMA of $0.43 and even further from its 200-day SMA of $0.69, indicating a prolonged downtrend.

The RSI reading of 38.00 places MATIC in neutral territory, suggesting the token is neither oversold nor overbought. This provides room for movement in either direction, making the next few trading sessions critical for determining short-term direction.

The MACD histogram at -0.0000 shows bearish momentum has stalled but hasn't yet turned positive. The convergence between MACD (-0.0246) and its signal line (-0.0246) suggests we may be approaching a potential momentum shift.

Polygon's position within the Bollinger Bands is particularly noteworthy. With a %B reading of 0.2879, MATIC trades closer to the lower band ($0.31) than the upper band ($0.56), indicating potential oversold conditions that could lead to a bounce.

In an optimistic scenario, MATIC could target the $0.43-$0.45 range, corresponding to the 20-day and 50-day moving averages. A successful break above $0.43 would signal the beginning of trend reversal, with the next major resistance at $0.45 representing a 18% upside from current levels.

For this Polygon forecast to materialize, MATIC would need to see increased buying volume and a decisive break above the middle Bollinger Band at $0.43. A successful retest of this level as support would strengthen the bullish case significantly.

The downside risk for this MATIC price prediction centers around the $0.31 support level, which aligns with the lower Bollinger Band. A break below this level could trigger further selling pressure, potentially pushing Polygon toward the $0.25-$0.28 range.

Given that MATIC trades below all major moving averages, the path of least resistance remains to the downside until proven otherwise. The 200-day SMA at $0.69 serves as a stark reminder of how far the token has fallen from its previous levels.

For traders considering a MATIC position, a scaled approach appears most prudent given the current technical setup. An initial entry around current levels ($0.38) could be considered, with additional purchases planned near the $0.35 and $0.31 support zones.

Stop-loss placement below $0.30 would limit downside risk while allowing room for normal price fluctuation. The low daily ATR of $0.02 suggests MATIC isn't experiencing extreme volatility, making position sizing more predictable.

Risk management remains crucial, as Polygon's correlation with broader crypto market sentiment means external factors could quickly invalidate technical setups. Consider position sizing that allows for potential dollar-cost averaging if the support levels are tested.

This MATIC price prediction suggests Polygon remains in a critical consolidation phase with potential for a recovery toward $0.45 over the next 4-6 weeks. However, traders should remain cautious given the token's position below key moving averages and the broader uncertainty in crypto markets.

The most likely scenario sees MATIC trading between $0.31 and $0.45 in the coming month, with direction determined by broader market sentiment and any significant developments in Polygon's ecosystem. A break above $0.45 would signal a more substantial recovery, while failure to hold $0.31 could lead to further downside.

Disclaimer: Cryptocurrency price predictions are highly speculative and subject to extreme volatility. This analysis is for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Always conduct your own research and consider your risk tolerance before making investment decisions.

Dogecoin continues to consolidate around the $0.10 level as traders await the next directional move. With mixed technical signals and analyst targets pointing higher, this DOGE price prediction examines the key levels that could determine the meme coin's trajectory through 2026.

• Short-term target (1 week): $0.092 - $0.097 • Medium-term forecast (1 month): $0.09 - $0.11 range • Bullish breakout level: $0.10 (strong resistance) • Critical support: $0.09

While specific analyst predictions are limited in recent days, established forecasting platforms have provided concrete targets for Dogecoin. According to CoinCodex analysis from March 18, 2026, "Dogecoin is predicted to gain 0.00% and reach a price of $0.09206 tomorrow," suggesting near-term consolidation around current levels.

More optimistically, DigitalCoinPrice noted in their March 19 analysis that "Dogecoin is on its way to passing the $0.14 mark by the end of this year," representing a 40% upside from current levels. The platform highlighted DOGE's recent volatility, noting it "reached $0.42 when it dropped below $0.31 for the first time since December 2013."

On-chain metrics from major data platforms suggest mixed momentum, with trading volumes remaining elevated at $76.6 million on Binance spot markets over the past 24 hours.

The current technical picture for Dogecoin presents a neutral to slightly bearish setup. The RSI stands at 51.57, firmly in neutral territory, indicating neither overbought nor oversold conditions. This suggests DOGE has room to move in either direction without immediate momentum constraints.

The MACD histogram reads 0.0000 with bearish momentum, as the MACD line (-0.0007) remains below the signal line (-0.0007). This configuration typically indicates weakening upward momentum, though the flat histogram suggests the bearish pressure is not accelerating.

Dogecoin's position within the Bollinger Bands shows a %B reading of 0.6676, placing it closer to the upper band ($0.10) than the lower band ($0.09). The middle band aligns with the 20-period SMA at $0.09, which currently acts as immediate support.

Moving averages present a mixed picture with shorter-term averages (SMA 7 and EMA 12 both at $0.09) below the current price, while the 50-period SMA matches the current level at $0.10. The 200-period SMA sits significantly higher at $0.15, representing a major resistance level for any sustained rally.

In the bullish case, DOGE needs to decisively break above the $0.10 resistance level, which currently serves as both the upper Bollinger Band and immediate resistance. A successful break could target the next significant level around $0.11-$0.12.

The ultimate bullish target aligns with analyst forecasts of $0.14 by year-end. Technical confirmation would require sustained trading above $0.10 with increasing volume and RSI pushing above 60 to indicate strengthening momentum.

The bearish scenario sees DOGE failing to hold the $0.09 support level, which represents both the lower Bollinger Band and immediate support. A break below this level could trigger selling toward the $0.085-$0.09 range.

Risk factors include the current bearish MACD momentum and the significant gap between current prices and the 200-period SMA at $0.15, suggesting the longer-term trend remains challenged.

Based on the current technical setup, potential entry points include:

Conservative Entry: Wait for a pullback to the $0.09 support level, using the lower Bollinger Band as a guide. This approach offers better risk-reward with a clear stop-loss below $0.085.

Breakout Entry: Consider positions on a confirmed break above $0.10 with increased volume. This strategy targets the analyst forecast of $0.14 but requires strict stop-loss management below $0.095.

Risk management suggests position sizing should account for DOGE's historical volatility, with stop-losses placed below key technical levels rather than arbitrary percentages.

This Dogecoin forecast suggests a period of consolidation in the near term, with the potential for a breakout toward analyst targets of $0.14 by year-end. The neutral RSI provides flexibility for movement in either direction, while the bearish MACD momentum warrants caution for immediate bullish bets.

The DOGE price prediction hinges on the cryptocurrency's ability to reclaim and hold above the $0.10 resistance level. While analyst targets suggest significant upside potential, traders should remain mindful of the technical challenges and manage risk accordingly.
Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/20260325-price-prediction-doge-targets-014-by-year-end-amid
